title: Define RepoBrief Agent Workbench boundary
goal: Document that RepoBrief may host deterministic read-only Agent Workbench code-understanding tools while patch/test/sandbox authority belongs to an external Patch Evaluation Sidecar.

acceptance.assertion: The boundary document is created or explicitly closed against current main/PR state with no hidden broadening.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: RepoBrief boundaries are preserved: no hidden refresh, no Git mutation in RepoBrief core, no patch/test execution inside the read-only evidence layer.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: The document states what Workbench evidence does not establish.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

metadata.outcome: Lenskit PR #891 documented the RepoBrief Agent Workbench boundary and corrected the term split: Agent Workbench is integrated deterministic code-understanding; Patch Evaluation Sidecar owns mutable patch/test/sandbox authority.

checks: GitHub PR #891 checks -> all reported checks passed
tip-to-tip diff origin/main..repobrief-agent-workbench-boundary-v1 -> 0 bytes after merge verification
acceptance_summary: boundary document exists on Lenskit main
RepoBrief remains deterministic evidence/snapshot/citation and read-only code-understanding infrastructure
Agent Workbench is defined as integrated deterministic read-only code-understanding tooling
Patch Evaluation Sidecar is defined as the external mutable patch/test/sandbox authority
non-claims explicitly cover correctness, test sufficiency, review completeness, merge readiness, security correctness and repo-understood claims
